brief_justification: Arrives at exact correct answer (B) with precise citation of Cohen's d benchmarks and clear rejection of the 'large effect' framing; explores three independent lines of evidence (d value, HDRS delta, NNT interpretation) and traces the statistical-vs-clinical significance distinction two levels deep; proceeds linearly without naming potential interpretive biases or questioning its own benchmark application; cross-validates the effect size against the report's own language and multiple metrics, though stops short of a counterfactual or boundary test; correctly separates statistical significance from clinical magnitude but does not identify what threshold or evidence would change the conclusion; implicitly addresses why A and D are tempting but does not name or systematically reject option C; every conclusion maps to a cited number or established benchmark, making the chain fully reproducible by a third party.
